219→
220→    @Option(
221→        names = ["--log-level", "-l"],
222→        description = ["Defines the minimum log level (trace, debug, info, warn, error) or none to suppress all logging"],
223→        converter = [LogLevelConverter::class],
224→    )
225→    private var minLogLevel: Level = Level.INFO
226→
227→    @Option(
228→        hidden = true,
229→        names = ["--disable-kotlin-extension-point"],
230→    )
231→    var disableKotlinExtensionPoint: Boolean = false
232→
233→    private val tripped = AtomicBoolean()
234→    private val fileNumber = AtomicInteger()
235→    private val errorNumber = AtomicInteger()
236→    private val adviseToUseFormat = AtomicBoolean()
237→
238→    internal var debug: Boolean = false
239→        get() = Level.DEBUG.isGreaterOrEqual(minLogLevel)
240→        private set
241→
242→    private fun disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides() =
243→        disabledRules
244→            .split(",")
245→            .filter { it.isNotBlank() }
246→            .map {
247→                // For backwards compatibility, prefix the rule id with the standard rule set id when missing
248→                RuleId.prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(it)
249→            }.map { RuleId(it).createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled }
250→            .toTypedArray()
251→
252→    fun run() {
253→        val editorConfigOverride =
254→            EditorConfigOverride
255→                .EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE
256→                .applyIf(experimental) {
257→                    logger.debug { "Add editor config override to allow the experimental rule set" }
258→                    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)
259→                }.applyIf(disabledRules.isNotBlank()) {
260→                    logger.debug { "Add editor config override to disable rules: '$disabledRules'" }
261→                    plus(*disabledRulesEditorConfigOverrides())
262→                }.applyIf(codeStyle == CodeStyleValue.android_studio) {
263→                    logger.debug { "Add editor config override to set code style to 'android_studio'" }
264→                    plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY to CodeStyleValue.android_studio)
265→                }.applyIf(stdin) {
266→                    logger.debug {
267→                        "Add editor config override to disable 'filename' rule which can not be used in combination with reading from " +
268→                            "<stdin>"
269→                    }
270→                    plus(FILENAME_RULE_ID.createRuleExecutionEditorConfigProperty() to RuleExecution.disabled)
271→                }
272→
273→        assertStdinAndPatternsFromStdinOptionsMutuallyExclusive()
274→        patterns = patterns.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty()
275→
276→        val start = System.currentTimeMillis()
277→
278→        val ruleProviders = ruleProviders()
279→        val ktLintRuleEngine =
280→            KtLintRuleEngine(
281→                ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
282→                editorConfigDefaults = editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders),
283→                editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
284→                isInvokedFromCli = true,
285→                enableKotlinCompilerExtensionPoint = !disableKotlinExtensionPoint,
286→            )
287→
288→        val baseline =
289→            if (stdin || baselinePath.isBlank()) {
290→                Baseline(status = Baseline.Status.DISABLED)
291→            } else {
292→                loadBaseline(baselinePath)
293→            }
294→        val aggregatedReporter =
295→            ReporterAggregator(
296→                baseline,
297→                reporterConfigurations,
298→                color,
299→                colorName,
300→                stdin,
301→                format,
302→                relative,
303→            ).aggregatedReporter()
304→
305→        aggregatedReporter.beforeAll()
306→        if (stdin) {
307→            lintStdin(
308→                ktLintRuleEngine,
309→                aggregatedReporter,
310→            )
311→        } else {
312→            lintFiles(
313→                ktLintRuleEngine,
314→                baseline.lintErrorsPerFile,
315→                aggregatedReporter,
316→            )
317→            if (adviseToUseFormat.get()) {
318→                if (format) {
319→                    logger.error { "Format was not able to autocorrect all errors that theoretically can be autocorrected." }
320→                } else {
321→                    logger.warn { "Lint has found errors than can be autocorrected using 'ktlint --format'" }
322→                }
323→            }
324→        }
325→        aggregatedReporter.afterAll()
326→
327→        logger.debug {
328→            "Finished processing in ${System.currentTimeMillis() - start}ms / $fileNumber file(s) scanned / $errorNumber error(s) found"
329→        }
330→        if (fileNumber.get() == 0) {
331→            // Do not return an error as this would implicate that in a multi-module project, each module has to contain
332→            // at least one kotlin file.
333→            logger.warn { "No files matched $patterns" }
334→        }
335→        if (tripped.get()) {
336→            exitKtLintProcess(1)
337→        } else {
338→            exitKtLintProcess(0)
339→        }
340→    }
341→
342→    private fun editorConfigDefaults(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigDefaults {
343→        val fullyExpandedEditorConfigPath =
344→            editorConfigPath
345→                ?.expandTildeToFullPath()
346→                ?.let { path -> Paths.get(path) }
347→        return EditorConfigDefaults.load(fullyExpandedEditorConfigPath, ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
348→    }
349→
350→    private fun List<String>.replaceWithPatternsFromStdinOrDefaultPatternsWhenEmpty(): List<String> {
351→        val localStdinDelimiter: String? = stdinDelimiter
352→        return when {
353→            localStdinDelimiter != null -> {
354→                val stdinPatterns: Set<String> = readPatternsFromStdin(localStdinDelimiter.ifEmpty { "\u0000" })
355→                if (isNotEmpty() && stdinPatterns.isNotEmpty()) {
356→                    logger.warn {
357→                        "Patterns specified at command line ($this) and patterns from 'stdin' due to flag '--patterns-from-stdin' " +
358→                            "($stdinPatterns) are merged"
359→                    }
360→                }
361→                // Note: it is okay in case both the original patterns and the patterns from stdin are empty
362→                this.plus(stdinPatterns)
363→            }
364→            this.isEmpty() -> {
365→                logger.info { "Enable default patterns $DEFAULT_PATTERNS" }
366→                DEFAULT_PATTERNS
367→            }
368→            else -> {
369→                // Keep original patterns
370→                this
371→            }
372→        }
373→    }
374→
375→    // Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
376→    internal fun ruleProviders(): Set<RuleProvider> = loadRuleProviders(rulesetJarPaths.toFilesURIList())
377→
378→    // Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
379→    internal fun configureLogger() {
380→        logger =
381→            KotlinLogging
382→                .logger {}
383→                .setDefaultLoggerModifier { logger ->
384→                    (logger.underlyingLogger as Logger).level = minLogLevel
385→                }
386→                .initKtLintKLogger()
387→    }
388→
389→    private fun assertStdinAndPatternsFromStdinOptionsMutuallyExclusive() {
390→        if (stdin && stdinDelimiter != null) {
391→            throw ParameterException(
392→                commandSpec.commandLine(),
393→                "Options --stdin and --patterns-from-stdin are mutually exclusive",
394→            )
395→        }
396→    }
397→
398→    private fun lintFiles(
399→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
400→        lintErrorsPerFile: Map<String, List<KtlintCliError>>,
401→        reporter: ReporterV2,
402→    ) {
403→        FileSystems
404→            .getDefault()
405→            .fileSequence(patterns)
406→            .map { it.toFile() }
407→            .takeWhile { errorNumber.get() < limit }
408→            .map { file ->
409→                Callable {
410→                    file to
411→                        process(
412→                            ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
413→                            code = Code.fromFile(file),
414→                            baselineLintErrors =
415→                                lintErrorsPerFile
416→                                    .getOrDefault(
417→                                        // Baseline stores the lint violations as relative path to work dir
418→                                        file.location(true),
419→                                        emptyList(),
420→                                    ),
421→                        )
422→                }
423→            }.parallel({ (file, errList) -> report(file.location(relative), errList, reporter) })
424→    }
425→
426→    private fun lintStdin(
427→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
428→        reporter: ReporterV2,
429→    ) {
430→        report(
431→            KtLintRuleEngine.STDIN_FILE,
432→            process(
433→                ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
434→                code = Code.fromStdin(),
435→                baselineLintErrors = emptyList(),
436→            ),
437→            reporter,
438→        )
439→    }
440→
441→    private fun report(
442→        relativeRoute: String,
443→        ktlintCliErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
444→        reporter: ReporterV2,
445→    ) {
446→        fileNumber.incrementAndGet()
447→        val errListLimit = minOf(ktlintCliErrors.size, maxOf(limit - errorNumber.get(), 0))
448→        errorNumber.addAndGet(errListLimit)
449→        if (!adviseToUseFormat.get() && ktlintCliErrors.containsErrorThatCanBeAutocorrected()) {
450→            adviseToUseFormat.set(true)
451→        }
452→
453→        reporter.before(relativeRoute)
454→        ktlintCliErrors
455→            .take(errListLimit)
456→            .forEach { reporter.onLintError(relativeRoute, it) }
457→        reporter.after(relativeRoute)
458→    }
459→
460→    private fun List<KtlintCliError>.containsErrorThatCanBeAutocorrected() = any { it.status == LINT_CAN_BE_AUTOCORRECTED }
461→
462→    private fun process(
463→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
464→        code: Code,
465→        baselineLintErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
466→    ): List<KtlintCliError> {
467→        if (code.fileName != null) {
468→            logger.trace { "Checking ${File(code.fileName!!).location(relative)}" }
469→        }
470→        return if (format) {
471→            format(ktLintRuleEngine, code, baselineLintErrors)
472→        } else {
473→            lint(ktLintRuleEngine, code, baselineLintErrors)
474→        }
475→    }
476→
477→    private fun format(
478→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
479→        code: Code,
480→        baselineLintErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
481→    ): List<KtlintCliError> {
482→        val ktlintCliErrors = mutableListOf<KtlintCliError>()
483→        try {
484→            ktLintRuleEngine
485→                .format(code) { lintError, corrected ->
486→                    val ktlintCliError =
487→                        KtlintCliError(
488→                            line = lintError.line,
489→                            col = lintError.col,
490→                            ruleId = lintError.ruleId.value,
491→                            detail =
492→                                lintError
493→                                    .detail
494→                                    .applyIf(corrected) { "$this (cannot be auto-corrected)" },
495→                            status =
496→                                if (corrected) {
497→                                    FORMAT_IS_AUTOCORRECTED
498→                                } else {
499→                                    LINT_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
500→                                },
501→                        )
502→                    if (baselineLintErrors.doesNotContain(ktlintCliError)) {
503→                        ktlintCliErrors.add(ktlintCliError)
504→                        if (!corrected) {
505→                            tripped.set(true)
506→                        }
507→                    }
508→                }.also { formattedFileContent ->
509→                    when {
510→                        code.isStdIn -> print(formattedFileContent)
511→                        code.content != formattedFileContent ->
512→                            code
513→                                .filePath
514→                                ?.toFile()
515→                                ?.writeText(formattedFileContent, charset("UTF-8"))
516→                    }
517→                }
518→        } catch (e: Exception) {
519→            if (code.isStdIn && e is KtLintParseException) {
520→                logger.warn {
521→                    """
522→                    Can not parse input from <stdin> as Kotlin, due to error below:
523→                        ${e.toKtlintCliError(code).detail}
524→                    Now, trying to read the input as Kotlin Script.
525→                    """.trimIndent()
526→                }
527→                return format(
528→                    ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
529→                    code = Code.fromSnippet(code.content, script = true),
530→                    baselineLintErrors = baselineLintErrors,
531→                )
532→            } else {
533→                ktlintCliErrors.add(e.toKtlintCliError(code))
534→                tripped.set(true)
535→                code.content // making sure `cat file | ktlint --stdin > file` is (relatively) safe
536→            }
537→        }
538→        return ktlintCliErrors.toList()
539→    }
540→
541→    private fun lint(
542→        ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
543→        code: Code,
544→        baselineLintErrors: List<KtlintCliError>,
545→    ): List<KtlintCliError> {
546→        val ktlintCliErrors = mutableListOf<KtlintCliError>()
547→        try {
548→            ktLintRuleEngine.lint(code) { lintError ->
549→                val ktlintCliError =
550→                    KtlintCliError(
551→                        line = lintError.line,
552→                        col = lintError.col,
553→                        ruleId = lintError.ruleId.value,
554→                        detail = lintError.detail,
555→                        status =
556→                            if (lintError.canBeAutoCorrected) {
557→                                LINT_CAN_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
558→                            } else {
559→                                LINT_CAN_NOT_BE_AUTOCORRECTED
560→                            },
561→                    )
562→                if (baselineLintErrors.doesNotContain(ktlintCliError)) {
563→                    ktlintCliErrors.add(ktlintCliError)
564→                    tripped.set(true)
565→                }
566→            }
567→        } catch (e: Exception) {
568→            if (code.isStdIn && e is KtLintParseException) {
569→                logger.warn {
570→                    """
571→                    Can not parse input from <stdin> as Kotlin, due to error below:
572→                        ${e.toKtlintCliError(code).detail}
573→                    Now, trying to read the input as Kotlin Script.
574→                    """.trimIndent()
575→                }
576→                return lint(
577→                    ktLintRuleEngine = ktLintRuleEngine,
578→                    code = Code.fromSnippet(code.content, script = true),
579→                    baselineLintErrors = baselineLintErrors,
580→                )
581→            } else {
582→                ktlintCliErrors.add(e.toKtlintCliError(code))
583→                tripped.set(true)
584→            }
585→        }
586→        return ktlintCliErrors.toList()
587→    }
588→
589→    private fun Exception.toKtlintCliError(code: Code): KtlintCliError =
590→        this.let { e ->
591→            when (e) {
592→                is KtLintParseException ->
593→                    KtlintCliError(
594→                        line = e.line,
595→                        col = e.col,
596→                        ruleId = "",
597→                        detail = "Not a valid Kotlin file (${e.message?.lowercase(Locale.getDefault())})",
598→                        status = KOTLIN_PARSE_EXCEPTION,
599→                    )
600→                is KtLintRuleException -> {
601→                    logger.debug("Internal Error (${e.ruleId}) in ${code.fileNameOrStdin()} at position '${e.line}:${e.col}", e)
602→                    KtlintCliError(
603→                        line = e.line,
604→                        col = e.col,
605→                        ruleId = "",
606→                        detail =
607→                            "Internal Error (rule '${e.ruleId}') in ${code.fileNameOrStdin()} at position '${e.line}:${e.col}. Please " +
608→                                "create a ticket at https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues and provide the source code that " +
609→                                "triggered an error.\n" +
610→                                e.stackTraceToString(),
611→                        status = KTLINT_RULE_ENGINE_EXCEPTION,
612→                    )
613→                }
614→                else -> throw e
615→            }
616→        }
617→
618→    private fun readPatternsFromStdin(delimiter: String): Set<String> {
619→        require(delimiter.isNotEmpty())
620→
621→        return String(System.`in`.readBytes())
622→            .split(delimiter)
623→            .let { patterns: List<String> ->
624→                patterns.filterTo(LinkedHashSet(patterns.size), String::isNotEmpty)
625→            }
626→    }
627→
628→    /**
629→     * Executes "Callable"s in parallel (lazily).
630→     * The results are gathered one-by-one (by `cb(<callable result>)`) in the order of corresponding "Callable"s
631→     * in the "Sequence" (think `seq.toList().map { executorService.submit(it) }.forEach { cb(it.get()) }` but without
632→     * buffering an entire sequence).
633→     *
634→     * Once kotlinx-coroutines are out of "experimental" stage everything below can be replaced with
635→     * ```
636→     * suspend fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(...) {
637→     *     val ctx = newFixedThreadPoolContext(numberOfThreads, "Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel")
638→     *     ctx.use {
639→     *         val channel = produce(ctx, numberOfThreads) {
640→     *             for (task in this@parallel) {
641→     *                 send(async(ctx) { task.call() })
642→     *             }
643→     *         }
644→     *         for (res in channel) {
645→     *             cb(res.await())
646→     *         }
647→     *     }
648→     * }
649→     * ```
650→     */
651→    private fun <T> Sequence<Callable<T>>.parallel(
652→        cb: (T) -> Unit,
653→        numberOfThreads: Int = Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors(),
654→    ) {
655→        val pill =
656→            object : Future<T> {
657→                override fun isDone(): Boolean {
658→                    throw UnsupportedOperationException()
659→                }
660→
661→                override fun get(
662→                    timeout: Long,
663→                    unit: TimeUnit,
664→                ): T {
665→                    throw UnsupportedOperationException()
666→                }
667→
668→                override fun get(): T {
669→                    throw UnsupportedOperationException()
670→                }
671→
672→                override fun cancel(mayInterruptIfRunning: Boolean): Boolean {
673→                    throw UnsupportedOperationException()
674→                }
675→
676→                override fun isCancelled(): Boolean {
677→                    throw UnsupportedOperationException()
678→                }
679→            }
680→        val q = ArrayBlockingQueue<Future<T>>(numberOfThreads)
681→        val producer =
682→            thread(start = true) {
683→                val executorService = Executors.newCachedThreadPool()
684→                try {
685→                    for (task in this) {
686→                        q.put(executorService.submit(task))
687→                    }
688→                    q.put(pill)
689→                } catch (e: InterruptedException) {
690→                    // we've been asked to stop consuming sequence
691→                } finally {
692→                    executorService.shutdown()
693→                }
694→            }
695→        try {
696→            while (true) {
697→                val result = q.take()
698→                if (result != pill) cb(result.get()) else break
699→            }
700→        } finally {
701→            producer.interrupt() // in case q.take()/result.get() throws
702→            producer.join()
703→        }
704→    }
705→}
706→
707→private class CodeStyleValueConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<CodeStyleValue> {
708→    @Throws(Exception::class)
709→    override fun convert(value: String?): CodeStyleValue =
710→        when (value?.lowercase()?.replace("-", "_")) {
711→            null -> CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
712→            "ktlint_official" -> CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official
713→            "android_studio" -> CodeStyleValue.android_studio
714→            "intellij_idea" -> CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea
715→            else -> throw IllegalArgumentException("Invalid code style value")
716→        }
717→}
718→
719→private class LogLevelConverter : CommandLine.ITypeConverter<Level> {
720→    @Throws(Exception::class)
721→    override fun convert(value: String?): Level =
722→        when (value?.uppercase()) {
723→            "TRACE" -> Level.TRACE
724→            "DEBUG" -> Level.DEBUG
725→            "INFO" -> Level.INFO
726→            "WARN" -> Level.WARN
727→            "ERROR" -> Level.ERROR
728→            "NONE" -> Level.OFF
729→            else -> throw IllegalStateException("Invalid log level '$value'")
730→        }
731→}
732→
733→// Do not convert to "val" as the function depends on PicoCli options which are not fully instantiated until the "run" method is started
734→internal fun List<String>.toFilesURIList() =
735→    map {
736→        val jarFile = File(it.expandTildeToFullPath())
737→        if (!jarFile.exists()) {
738→            logger.error { "File '$it' does not exist" }
739→            exitKtLintProcess(1)
740→        }
741→        jarFile.toURI().toURL()
742→    }
743→
744→/**
745→ * Wrapper around exitProcess which ensure that a proper log line is written which can be used in unit tests for
746→ * validating the result of the test.
747→ */
748→internal fun exitKtLintProcess(status: Int): Nothing {
749→    logger.debug { "Exit ktlint with exit code: $status" }
750→    exitProcess(status)
751→}
752→
753→/**
754→ * Gets the relative route of the path. Also adjusts the slashes for uniformity between file systems.
755→ */
756→internal val Path.relativeRoute: String
757→    get() {
758→        val rootPath = Paths.get("").toAbsolutePath()
759→        return this
760→            .relativeToOrSelf(rootPath)
761→            .pathString
762→            .replace(File.separatorChar, '/')
763→    }
764→

conversation_history.content:
1→In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 2→
 3→```ini
 4→[*.{kt,kts}]
 5→ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 6→```
 7→
 8→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.
 9→
10→!!! note
11→    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
12→
13→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
14→
15→* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Be aware that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
16→
17→* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
18→

214→    @Test
215→    fun `Given an editorconfig containing a property for which the value is unset then return its default value`() {
216→        val editorConfig =
217→            EditorConfig(
218→                SOME_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY.toPropertyWithValue("unset"),
219→            )
220→
221→        val actual = editorConfig[SOME_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY]
222→
223→        assertThat(actual).isEqualTo(SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA)
224→    }
225→
226→    @Test
227→    fun `Given an editorconfig containing a property with an invalid source value then return its default value`() {
228→        val someEditorConfigProperty = editorConfigProperty("some-editor-config-property")
229→        val editorConfig =
230→            EditorConfig(
231→                someEditorConfigProperty.toPropertyWithValue("some-invalid-value"),
232→            )
233→
234→        val actual = editorConfig[someEditorConfigProperty]
235→
236→        assertThat(actual).isEqualTo(someEditorConfigProperty.defaultValue)
237→    }
238→
239→    @Test
240→    fun `Given an editorconfig containing a property with undefined type then retrieving that property via the EditConfig may not result in an exception`() {
241→        // The Property and PropertyValue builders of the ec4j library do not allow to build a property having field "Property.type" set to
242→        // null. However, when loading an ".editorconfig" file containing a property with a name for which no property type exists with that
243→        // same name, this does result in such a property. It takes a detour to create the property:
244→        //  - Create an ".editorconfig" file on the file system mock
245→        //  - Load the ".editorconfig" via the EditorConfigDefaults loader
246→        //  - Find the property which is set in the ".editorconfig" file and validate that its type is null
247→        //  - Transform the property to a KtLint EditorConfig
248→        //  - Extract the property of the KtLint EditorConfig
249→        // Note that two properties are created which are identical except that only one of the properties has a null type and the other
250→        // property has a correctly defined type.
251→        //language=
252→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = "ktlint_test_rule-1"
253→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1)
254→        //language=
255→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = "ktlint_test_rule-2"
256→        val ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2 = editorConfigProperty(ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2)
257→
258→        val ktlintTestFileSystem =
259→            KtlintTestFileSystem().apply {
260→                writeRootEditorConfigFile(
261→                    //language=EditorConfig
262→                    """
263→                    [*.{kt,kts}]
264→                    $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1 = disabled
265→                    $ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2 = disabled
266→                    """.trimIndent(),
267→                )
268→            }
269→
270→        val ktlintTestRuleProperties =
271→            EditorConfigDefaults
272→                .load(
273→                    path = ktlintTestFileSystem.resolve(""),
274→                    propertyTypes =
275→                        setOf(
276→                            // Note that ktlintTestRuleEditorConfigPropertyType1 has been left out on purpose
277→                            ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2.type,
278→                        ),
279→                ).value
280→                .sections
281→                .flatMap { it.properties.values }
282→                .associateBy { it.name }
283→
284→        // Validate that both properties are loaded
285→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1] != null) {
286→            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
287→        }
288→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2] != null) {
289→            "Can not find property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' in the '.editorconfig' file on the file system mock"
290→        }
291→
292→        // Validate that the type of one of the properties is null
293→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1]?.type == null) {
294→            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty1' should have an undefined type"
295→        }
296→        require(ktlintTestRuleProperties[ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2]?.type != null) {
297→            "Property '$ktlintTestRuleExecutionProperty2' should have a defined type"
298→        }
299→
300→        val editorConfig = EditorConfig(ktlintTestRuleProperties)
301→
302→        // Although the type of one of the properties is null, both can be loaded as the type of EditorConfigProperty is used to parse the
303→        // raw value of the property
304→        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType1]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
305→        assertThat(editorConfig[ktlintTestRuleExecutionPropertyType2]).isEqualTo(RuleExecution.disabled)
306→    }
307→
308→    private fun editorConfigProperty(name: String) =
309→        EditorConfigProperty(
310→            name = name,
311→            type =
312→                PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
313→                    name,
314→                    "",
315→                    SafeEnumValueParser(RuleExecution::class.java),
316→                    RuleExecution.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
317→                ),
318→            defaultValue = RuleExecution.enabled,
319→        )
320→
321→    private companion object {
322→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_NAME = "some-property-name"
323→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE = "some-property-value"
324→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO = "some-property-value-android"
325→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT = "some-property-value-default"
326→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA = "some-property-value-intellij-idea"
327→        const val SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL = "some-property-value-official"
328→        val SOME_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTY =
329→            EditorConfigProperty(
330→                name = SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
331→                type =
332→                    PropertyType(
333→                        SOME_PROPERTY_NAME,
334→                        "",
335→                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.IDENTITY_VALUE_PARSER,
336→                        setOf(SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO, SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA),
337→                    ),
338→                defaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_DEFAULT,
339→                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_ANDROID_STUDIO,
340→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_OFFICIAL,
341→                intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = SOME_PROPERTY_VALUE_INTELLIJ_IDEA,
342→            )
343→    }
344→}
345→

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
  2→
  3→!!! danger
  4→
  5→    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
  6→
  7→## Code style
  8→
  9→By default, the `intellij_idea` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `ktlint_official` or `android`.
 10→
 11→```ini
 12→[*.{kt,kts}]
 13→ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 14→```
 15→
 16→!!! note
 17→    The default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` version of ktlint.
 18→
 19→## Disabled rules
 20→
 21→!!! note
 22→    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
 23→
 24→Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
 25→
 26→All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 27→```editorconfig
 28→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 29→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
 30→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 31→```
 32→
 33→Rules that are marked as experimental will not be run, unless explicitly enabled:
 34→```editorconfig
 35→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 36→```
 37→
 38→An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 39→```editorconfig
 40→ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
 41→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 42→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 43→```
 44→
 45→!!! note
 46→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 47→
 48→
 49→## Final newline
 50→
 51→By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
 52→
 53→```ini
 54→[*.{kt,kts}]
 55→insert_final_newline = true
 56→```
 57→
 58→This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
 59→
 60→## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters
 61→
 62→Setting `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` forces a multiline function signature in case the function contains the specified minimum number of parameters even in case the function signature would fit on a single line. Use value `unset` (default) to disable this setting.
 63→
 64→!!! note
 65→    By default, the `ktlint_official` code style wraps parameters of functions having at least 2 parameters. For other code styles, this setting is disabled by default. 
 66→
 67→```ini
 68→[*.{kt,kts}]
 69→ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than=unset
 70→```
 71→
 72→This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
 73→
 74→## Wrapping the expression body of a function
 75→
 76→Setting `ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping` determines if and when the expression body of a function is wrapped to a new line. This setting can be set to value `default`, `multiline` or `always`. 
 77→
 78→When set to `default`, the first line of a body expression is appended to the function signature as long as the max line length is not exceeded.
 79→
 80→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=default (or when not set)"
 81→// Given that the function signature has to be written as a single line function signature
 82→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 83→    .uppercase()
 84→
 85→// Given that the function signature has to be written as a multiline function signature
 86→fun someFunction(
 87→    a: Any,
 88→    b: Any
 89→): String = "some-result"
 90→    .uppercase()
 91→```
 92→
 93→When set to `multiline`, the body expression starts on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
 94→
 95→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=multiline"
 96→// Given a single line body expression and
 97→// a the function signature that has to be written as a single line function signature and
 98→// it does not exceed the max line length
 99→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result".uppercase()
100→
101→// Given a single line body expression and
102→// a the function signature that has to be written as a multiline function signature and
103→// it does not exceed the max line length
104→fun someFunction(
105→    a: Any,
106→    b: Any
107→): String = "some-result".uppercase()
108→
109→// Given a single line body expression then always wrap it to a separate line
110→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
111→    "some-result"
112→         .uppercase()
113→fun someFunction(
114→    a: Any,
115→    b: Any
116→): String =
117→    "some-result"
118→       .uppercase()
119→```
120→
121→When set to `always` the body expression is always wrapped to a separate line.
122→
123→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=always"
124→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
125→    "some-result".uppercase()
126→fun functionWithAVeryLongName(
127→    a: Any,
128→    b: Any
129→): String =
130→    "some-result"
131→        .uppercase()
132→```
133→
134→This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
135→
136→## Ignore identifiers enclosed in backticks
137→
138→By default, the identifiers enclosed in backticks are not ignored.
139→
140→According to [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) it is acceptable to write method names in natural language. When using natural language, the description tends to be longer. This property allows lines containing an identifier between backticks to be longer than the maximum line length. (Since 0.41.0)
141→
142→```kotlin
143→@Test
144→fun `Given a test with a very loooooooooooooooooooooong test description`() {
145→    
146→}
147→```
148→
149→```ini
150→[*.{kt,kts}]
151→ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier = false
152→```
153→
154→This setting only takes effect when rule `max-line-length` is enabled.
155→
156→## Import layouts
157→
158→By default, the same imports are allowed as in IntelliJ IDEA. The import path can be a full path, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard path, e.g. "kotlin.**".
159→
160→The layout can be composed by the following symbols:
161→
162→*  `*` - wildcard. There must be at least one entry of a single wildcard to match all other imports. Matches anything after a specified symbol/import as well.
163→* `|` - blank line. Supports only single blank lines between imports. No blank line is allowed in the beginning or end of the layout.
164→* `^` - alias import, e.g. "^android.*" will match all android alias imports, "^" will match all other alias imports.
165→
166→Examples:
167→```kotlin
168→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=* # alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a), no blank lines
169→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^ # default IntelliJ IDEA style, same as alphabetical, but with "java", "javax", "kotlin" and alias imports in the end of the imports list
170→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=android.**,|,^org.junit.**,kotlin.io.Closeable.*,|,*,^ # custom imports layout
171→```
172→
173→Wildcard imports can be allowed for specific import paths (Comma-separated list, use "**" as wildcard for package and all subpackages). This setting overrides the no-wildcard-imports rule. This setting is best be used for allowing wildcard imports from libraries like Ktor where extension functions are used in a way that creates a lot of imports.
174→
175→```ini
176→[*.{kt,kts}]
177→ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**
178→```
179→
180→This setting only takes effect when rule `no-wildcard-imports` is enabled.
181→
182→## Indent size & style
183→
184→By default, indenting is done with 4 spaces per indent level. Code style `android_studio` uses a tab per indent level.
185→
186→```ini
187→[*.{kt,kts}]
188→indent_size = 4 # possible values: number (e.g. 2), "unset" (makes ktlint ignore indentation completely)  
189→indent_style = space # or "tab"
190→```
191→
192→Those settings are used by multiple rules of which rule `indent` is the most important.
193→
194→## Max line length
195→
196→By default, the maximum line length is not set. The `android` code style sets the max line length to 100 (per Android Kotlin Style Guide).
197→
198→```ini
199→[*.{kt,kts}]
200→max_line_length = off # Use "off" to ignore max line length or a positive number to set max line length
201→```
202→
203→This setting is used by multiple rules of which rule `max-line-length` is the most important.
204→
205→## Trailing comma on call site
206→
207→KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at call site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
208→
209→!!! note
210→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
211→
212→    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing commas on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
213→    
214→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
215→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
216→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
217→
218→Example:
219→```ini
220→[*.{kt,kts}]
221→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = false
222→```
223→
224→This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-call-site` is enabled.
225→
226→## Trailing comma on declaration site
227→
228→KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
229→
230→!!! note
231→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
232→
233→    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas) encourages the usage of trailing commas on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing commas on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
234→    
235→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
236→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
237→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
238→
239→Example:
240→```ini
241→[*.{kt,kts}]
242→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = false # Only used for declaration site 
243→```
244→
245→This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` is enabled.
246→
247→## Overriding Editorconfig properties for specific directories
248→
249→You can [override](https://editorconfig.org/#file-format-details) properties for specific directories inside your project:
250→```ini
251→[*.{kt,kts}]
252→ktlint_standard_import-ordering = disabled
253→
254→[api/*.{kt,kts}]
255→ktlint_standard_indent = disabled
256→```
257→
258→Note that the `import-ordering` rule is disabled for *all* packages including the `api` sub package. Next to this the `indent` rule is disabled for the `api` package and its sub packages.
259→

function.name: edit
function.arguments:
{"old_string":"In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).

```ini
[*.{kt,kts}]
ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
```

This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.

!!! note
    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.

The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.

* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Be aware that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.

* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.","file_path":"/project/documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/code-styles.md","new_string":"Starting from ktlint `1.0` the code style `ktlint_official` is the default code style. Please provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).

```ini
[*.{kt,kts}]
ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
```

This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.

!!! note
    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.

The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.

* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, `ktlint_official` is the default code style.

* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`."}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COMMA
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.INDICES
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isCodeLeaf
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextSibling
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeLeaf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevCodeSibling
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 22→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 23→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet
 27→
 28→/**
 29→ * Linting trailing comma for call site.
 30→ *
 31→ * @see [Kotlin Style Guide](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas)
 32→ */
 33→public class TrailingCommaOnCallSiteRule :
 34→    StandardRule(
 35→        id = "trailing-comma-on-call-site",
 36→        visitorModifiers =
 37→            setOf(
 38→                VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 39→                    ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 40→                    mode = ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED,
 41→                ),
 42→                VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 43→            ),
 44→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_PROPERTY),
 45→    ) {
 46→    private var allowTrailingCommaOnCallSite = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 47→
 48→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 49→        allowTrailingCommaOnCallSite = editorConfig[TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_PROPERTY]
 50→    }
 51→
 52→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 53→        node: ASTNode,
 54→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 55→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 56→    ) {
 57→        // Keep processing of element types in sync with Intellij Kotlin formatting settings.
 58→        // https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-kotlin/blob/master/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/formatter/trailingComma/util.kt
 59→        when (node.elementType) {
 60→            COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION -> visitCollectionLiteralExpression(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 61→            INDICES -> visitIndices(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 62→            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> visitTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 63→            VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> visitValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 64→            else -> Unit
 65→        }
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    private fun visitCollectionLiteralExpression(
 69→        node: ASTNode,
 70→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        val inspectNode =
 74→            node
 75→                .children()
 76→                .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RBRACKET }
 77→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
 78→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
 79→            emit = emit,
 80→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
 81→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
 82→        )
 83→    }
 84→
 85→    private fun ASTNode.isTrailingCommaAllowed() = elementType in TYPES_ON_CALL_SITE && allowTrailingCommaOnCallSite
 86→
 87→    private fun visitIndices(
 88→        node: ASTNode,
 89→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 90→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 91→    ) {
 92→        val inspectNode =
 93→            node
 94→                .children()
 95→                .last { it.elementType == ElementType.RBRACKET }
 96→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
 97→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
 98→            emit = emit,
 99→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
100→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
101→        )
102→    }
103→
104→    private fun visitValueList(
105→        node: ASTNode,
106→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
107→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
108→    ) {
109→        if (node.treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL) {
110→            node
111→                .children()
112→                .lastOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.RPAR }
113→                ?.let { inspectNode ->
114→                    node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
115→                        inspectNode = inspectNode,
116→                        emit = emit,
117→                        isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
118→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
119→                    )
120→                }
121→        }
122→    }
123→
124→    private fun visitTypeList(
125→        node: ASTNode,
126→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
128→    ) {
129→        val inspectNode =
130→            node
131→                .children()
132→                .first { it.elementType == ElementType.GT }
133→        node.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
134→            inspectNode = inspectNode,
135→            emit = emit,
136→            isTrailingCommaAllowed = node.isTrailingCommaAllowed(),
137→            autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
138→        )
139→    }
140→
141→    private fun ASTNode.reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore(
142→        inspectNode: ASTNode,
143→        isTrailingCommaAllowed: Boolean,
144→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
145→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
146→    ) {
147→        val prevLeaf = inspectNode.prevLeaf()
148→        val trailingCommaNode = prevLeaf?.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull()
149→        val trailingCommaState =
150→            when {
151→                this.isMultiline() -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.EXISTS else TrailingCommaState.MISSING
152→                else -> if (trailingCommaNode != null) TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT else TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS
153→            }
154→        when (trailingCommaState) {
155→            TrailingCommaState.EXISTS ->
156→                if (!isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
157→                    emit(
158→                        trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
159→                        "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
160→                        true,
161→                    )
162→                    if (autoCorrect) {
163→                        this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
164→                    }
165→                }
166→            TrailingCommaState.MISSING ->
167→                if (isTrailingCommaAllowed) {
168→                    val prevNode = inspectNode.prevCodeLeaf()!!
169→                    emit(
170→                        prevNode.startOffset + prevNode.textLength,
171→                        "Missing trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
172→                        true,
173→                    )
174→                    if (autoCorrect) {
175→                        inspectNode
176→                            .prevCodeSibling()
177→                            ?.nextSibling()
178→                            ?.let { before ->
179→                                before.treeParent.addChild(LeafPsiElement(COMMA, ","), before)
180→                            }
181→                    }
182→                }
183→            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {
184→                emit(
185→                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
186→                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
187→                    true,
188→                )
189→                if (autoCorrect) {
190→                    this.removeChild(trailingCommaNode)
191→                }
192→            }
193→            TrailingCommaState.NOT_EXISTS -> Unit
194→        }
195→    }
196→
197→    private fun ASTNode.isMultiline(): Boolean =
198→        if (elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
199→            hasAtLeastOneArgument() && hasValueArgumentFollowedByWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
200→        } else {
201→            textContains('\n')
202→        }
203→
204→    private fun ASTNode.hasValueArgumentFollowedByWhiteSpaceWithNewline(): Boolean =
205→        findValueArgumentFollowedByWhiteSpaceWithNewline() != null
206→
207→    private fun ASTNode.findValueArgumentFollowedByWhiteSpaceWithNewline() =
208→        this
209→            .findChildByType(VALUE_ARGUMENT)
210→            ?.nextSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
211→
212→    private fun ASTNode.hasAtLeastOneArgument() = children().any { it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT }
213→
214→    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
215→        val codeLeaf =
216→            if (isCodeLeaf()) {
217→                this
218→            } else {
219→                prevCodeLeaf()
220→            }
221→        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
222→    }
223→
224→    private enum class TrailingCommaState {
225→        /**
226→         * The trailing comma is needed and exists
227→         */
228→        EXISTS,
229→
230→        /**
231→         * The trailing comma is needed and doesn't exist
232→         */
233→        MISSING,
234→
235→        /**
236→         * The trailing comma isn't needed and doesn't exist
237→         */
238→        NOT_EXISTS,
239→
240→        /**
241→         * The trailing comma isn't needed, but exists
242→         */
243→        REDUNDANT,
244→    }
245→
246→    public companion object {
247→        private val BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET = setOf("true", "false")
248→
249→        public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Boolean> =
250→            EditorConfigProperty(
251→                type =
252→                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
253→                        "ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site",
254→                        "Defines whether a trailing comma (or no trailing comma) should be enforced on the calling side," +
255→                            "e.g. argument-list, when-entries, lambda-arguments, indices, etc." +
256→                            "When set, IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to allow usage of a trailing comma by discretion " +
257→                            "of the developer. KtLint however uses this setting to enforce consistent usage of the " +
258→                            "trailing comma when set.",
259→                        PropertyValueParser.BOOLEAN_VALUE_PARSER,
260→                        BOOLEAN_VALUES_SET,
261→                    ),
262→                defaultValue = true,
263→                androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = false,
264→            )
265→
266→        private val TYPES_ON_CALL_SITE =
267→            TokenSet.create(
268→                COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION,
269→                INDICES,
270→                TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST,
271→                VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST,
272→            )
273→    }
274→}
275→
276→public val TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID: RuleId = TrailingCommaOnCallSiteRule().ruleId
277→

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLON
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COMMA
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EQ
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.Companion.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.leavesIncludingSelf
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 26→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 27→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 28→import mu.KotlinLogging
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 30→
 31→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 32→
 33→/**
 34→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a value parameter.
 35→ *
 36→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 37→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 38→ * indentation rule is not run.
 39→ *
 40→ * This rule has many similarities with the [PropertyWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 41→ */
 42→public class ParameterWrappingRule :
 43→    StandardRule(
 44→        id = "parameter-wrapping",
 45→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 46→            setOf(
 47→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 48→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 49→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 50→            ),
 51→    ) {
 52→    private var line = 1
 53→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 54→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 55→
 56→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 57→        line = 1
 58→        indentConfig =
 59→            IndentConfig(
 60→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 61→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 62→            )
 63→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER) {
 72→            rearrangeValueParameter(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 73→        }
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    private fun rearrangeValueParameter(
 77→        node: ASTNode,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80→    ) {
 81→        require(node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 82→
 83→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 84→
 85→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 86→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 87→        val fromNode =
 88→            node
 89→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 90→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 91→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 92→                ?: node
 93→
 94→        node
 95→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 96→            ?.let { colon ->
 97→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 98→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 99→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
100→                    return
101→                }
102→            }
103→
104→        node
105→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
106→            ?.let { typeReference ->
107→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
108→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                    return
110→                }
111→            }
112→
113→        node
114→            .findChildByType(EQ)
115→            ?.let { equal ->
116→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
117→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
118→                    return
119→                }
120→            }
121→
122→        node
123→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
124→            ?.let { callExpression ->
125→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
126→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
127→                    return
128→                }
129→            }
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.orTrailingComma() =
133→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
134→            .nextCodeLeaf()
135→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == COMMA }
136→            ?: this
137→
138→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
139→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
140→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
141→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
142→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
143→    }
144→
145→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
146→        node: ASTNode,
147→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
148→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149→    ) {
150→        emit(
151→            node.startOffset - 1,
152→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
153→            true,
154→        )
155→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
156→        if (autoCorrect) {
157→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
162→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
163→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
164→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
165→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
166→    ) {
167→        emit(
168→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
169→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
170→            true,
171→        )
172→        LOGGER.trace {
173→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
174→        }
175→        if (autoCorrect) {
176→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
177→        }
178→    }
179→}
180→
181→public val PARAMETER_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ParameterWrappingRule().ruleId

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.SPACE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.TAB
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
  9→
 10→public data class IndentConfig(
 11→    val indentStyle: IndentStyle,
 12→    /**
 13→     * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 14→     */
 15→    val tabWidth: Int,
 16→) {
 17→    /**
 18→     * To use the [IndentConfig] in a rule, the following needs to be done:
 19→     * 1. Implement interface [UsesEditorConfigProperties] on the rule
 20→     * 2. Register the used or properties
 21→     *    ```
 22→     *    override val editorConfigProperties: List<UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 23→     *        listOf(indentSizeProperty, indentStyleProperty)
 24→     *    ```
 25→     * 3. Initialize the IndentConfig
 26→     *    ```
 27→     *    indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 28→     *        indentStyle = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentStyleProperty),
 29→     *        tabWidth = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentSizeProperty)
 30→     *    )
 31→     *    ```
 32→     */
 33→    public constructor(
 34→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 35→        /**
 36→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 37→         */
 38→        tabWidth: Int,
 39→    ) : this(
 40→        indentStyle =
 41→            when (indentStyle) {
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 43→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 44→            },
 45→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 49→
 50→    private val indentChar =
 51→        when (indentStyle) {
 52→            TAB -> '\t'
 53→            SPACE -> ' '
 54→        }
 55→
 56→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 57→        when (indentStyle) {
 58→            TAB -> ' '
 59→            SPACE -> '\t'
 60→        }
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 64→     * configuration.
 65→     */
 66→    public val disabled: Boolean
 67→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 68→
 69→    /**
 70→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 71→     * configuration.
 72→     */
 73→    public val indent: String =
 74→        if (disabled) {
 75→            ""
 76→        } else {
 77→            when (indentStyle) {
 78→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 79→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 80→            }
 81→        }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the next indent level compared to the given node.
 85→     */
 86→    public fun childIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.indent().plus(indent)
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the given node.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun siblingIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = parentIndentOf(node).plus(indent)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the parent of the given node.
 95→     */
 96→    public fun parentIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.treeParent.indent()
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
100→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
101→     *
102→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
103→     *
104→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
105→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
106→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
107→     */
108→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
109→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
110→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
111→        return when (indentStyle) {
112→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
113→            TAB -> {
114→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
115→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
116→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
117→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
118→            }
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
123→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
124→        val indent =
125→            if (index == -1) {
126→                text
127→            } else {
128→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
129→            }
130→        return indent
131→    }
132→
133→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
137→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
138→     *
139→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
140→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
141→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
142→     */
143→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
144→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
145→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
146→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
147→    }
148→
149→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
150→
151→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
152→
153→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
154→        when (indentStyle) {
155→            SPACE -> "tab"
156→            TAB -> "space"
157→        }
158→
159→    public companion object {
160→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
161→
162→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
163→            IndentConfig(
164→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
165→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
166→            )
167→    }
168→}
169→

 9→        name = PropertyType.indent_size.name,

24→                    }
