1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CALL_EXPRESSION
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLON
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COMMA
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.EQ
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_PARAMETER
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.Companion.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.firstChildLeafOrSelf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.indent
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 21→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lastChildLeafOrSelf
 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.leavesIncludingSelf
 23→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextCodeLeaf
 24→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 25→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 26→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe
 27→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 28→import mu.KotlinLogging
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 30→
 31→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 32→
 33→/**
 34→ * This rule inserts missing newlines inside a value parameter.
 35→ *
 36→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 37→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 38→ * indentation rule is not run.
 39→ *
 40→ * This rule has many similarities with the [PropertyWrappingRule] but some subtle differences.
 41→ */
 42→public class ParameterWrappingRule :
 43→    StandardRule(
 44→        id = "parameter-wrapping",
 45→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 46→            setOf(
 47→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 48→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 49→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 50→            ),
 51→    ) {
 52→    private var line = 1
 53→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 54→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 55→
 56→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 57→        line = 1
 58→        indentConfig =
 59→            IndentConfig(
 60→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 61→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 62→            )
 63→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 67→        node: ASTNode,
 68→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 69→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 70→    ) {
 71→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER) {
 72→            rearrangeValueParameter(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 73→        }
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    private fun rearrangeValueParameter(
 77→        node: ASTNode,
 78→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 79→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 80→    ) {
 81→        require(node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER)
 82→
 83→        val baseIndentLength = node.indent(false).length
 84→
 85→        // Find the first node after the indenting whitespace on the same line as the identifier
 86→        val nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf = node.firstChildLeafOrSelf()
 87→        val fromNode =
 88→            node
 89→                .findChildByType(ElementType.IDENTIFIER)
 90→                ?.leavesIncludingSelf(forward = false)
 91→                ?.firstOrNull { it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || it == nodeFirstChildLeafOrSelf }
 92→                ?: node
 93→
 94→        node
 95→            .findChildByType(COLON)
 96→            ?.let { colon ->
 97→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon) > maxLineLength) {
 98→                    fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(colon)
 99→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
100→                    return
101→                }
102→            }
103→
104→        node
105→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
106→            ?.let { typeReference ->
107→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(typeReference.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
108→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(typeReference, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                    return
110→                }
111→            }
112→
113→        node
114→            .findChildByType(EQ)
115→            ?.let { equal ->
116→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(equal.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
117→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(equal, autoCorrect, emit)
118→                    return
119→                }
120→            }
121→
122→        node
123→            .findChildByType(CALL_EXPRESSION)
124→            ?.let { callExpression ->
125→                if (baseIndentLength + fromNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(callExpression.orTrailingComma()) > maxLineLength) {
126→                    requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(callExpression, autoCorrect, emit)
127→                    return
128→                }
129→            }
130→    }
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.orTrailingComma() =
133→        lastChildLeafOrSelf()
134→            .nextCodeLeaf()
135→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == COMMA }
136→            ?: this
137→
138→    private fun ASTNode.sumOfTextLengthUntil(astNode: ASTNode): Int {
139→        val stopAtLeaf = astNode.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
140→        return leavesIncludingSelf()
141→            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() && it.prevLeaf() != stopAtLeaf }
142→            .sumOf { it.textLength }
143→    }
144→
145→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
146→        node: ASTNode,
147→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
148→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149→    ) {
150→        emit(
151→            node.startOffset - 1,
152→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
153→            true,
154→        )
155→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
156→        if (autoCorrect) {
157→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(node.indent())
158→        }
159→    }
160→
161→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
162→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
163→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
164→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
165→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
166→    ) {
167→        emit(
168→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
169→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
170→            true,
171→        )
172→        LOGGER.trace {
173→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
174→        }
175→        if (autoCorrect) {
176→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(nodeToFix))
177→        }
178→    }
179→}
180→
181→public val PARAMETER_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ParameterWrappingRule().ruleId

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.SPACE
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig.IndentStyle.TAB
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
  9→
 10→public data class IndentConfig(
 11→    val indentStyle: IndentStyle,
 12→    /**
 13→     * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 14→     */
 15→    val tabWidth: Int,
 16→) {
 17→    /**
 18→     * To use the [IndentConfig] in a rule, the following needs to be done:
 19→     * 1. Implement interface [UsesEditorConfigProperties] on the rule
 20→     * 2. Register the used or properties
 21→     *    ```
 22→     *    override val editorConfigProperties: List<UsesEditorConfigProperties.EditorConfigProperty<*>> =
 23→     *        listOf(indentSizeProperty, indentStyleProperty)
 24→     *    ```
 25→     * 3. Initialize the IndentConfig
 26→     *    ```
 27→     *    indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 28→     *        indentStyle = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentStyleProperty),
 29→     *        tabWidth = node.getEditorConfigValue(indentSizeProperty)
 30→     *    )
 31→     *    ```
 32→     */
 33→    public constructor(
 34→        indentStyle: PropertyType.IndentStyleValue,
 35→        /**
 36→         * The number of spaces that is equivalent to one tab
 37→         */
 38→        tabWidth: Int,
 39→    ) : this(
 40→        indentStyle =
 41→            when (indentStyle) {
 42→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.tab -> TAB
 43→                PropertyType.IndentStyleValue.space -> SPACE
 44→            },
 45→        tabWidth = tabWidth,
 46→    )
 47→
 48→    public enum class IndentStyle { SPACE, TAB }
 49→
 50→    private val indentChar =
 51→        when (indentStyle) {
 52→            TAB -> '\t'
 53→            SPACE -> ' '
 54→        }
 55→
 56→    private val unexpectedIndentChar =
 57→        when (indentStyle) {
 58→            TAB -> ' '
 59→            SPACE -> '\t'
 60→        }
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * When disabled, rules may not enforce any indentation related changes regardless of other settings in this
 64→     * configuration.
 65→     */
 66→    public val disabled: Boolean
 67→        get() = tabWidth <= 0
 68→
 69→    /**
 70→     * Normalized indent of 1 level deep. Representation is either in spaces or a single tab depending on the
 71→     * configuration.
 72→     */
 73→    public val indent: String =
 74→        if (disabled) {
 75→            ""
 76→        } else {
 77→            when (indentStyle) {
 78→                TAB -> indentChar.toString()
 79→                SPACE -> indentChar.toString().repeat(tabWidth)
 80→            }
 81→        }
 82→
 83→    /**
 84→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the next indent level compared to the given node.
 85→     */
 86→    public fun childIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.indent().plus(indent)
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the given node.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun siblingIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = parentIndentOf(node).plus(indent)
 92→
 93→    /**
 94→     * Get the indentation including the newline character for a node at the same indent level as the parent of the given node.
 95→     */
 96→    public fun parentIndentOf(node: ASTNode): String = node.treeParent.indent()
 97→
 98→    /**
 99→     * Converts [text] to a normalized indent. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
100→     * is converted. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
101→     *
102→     * Tabs in the given text are converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style.
103→     *
104→     * When converting a text (possibly contains both tabs and spaces) to tabs, all tabs are first converted to
105→     * spaces. Each consecutive set of spaces is converted to tabs. Sapces which are left, e.g. not enough space to
106→     * complete a full tab, are ignored silently.
107→     */
108→    public fun toNormalizedIndent(text: String): String {
109→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
110→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
111→        return when (indentStyle) {
112→            SPACE -> indent.replaceTabWithSpaces()
113→            TAB -> {
114→                "\t".repeat(indentLevelFrom(indent))
115→                // Silently swallow spaces if not enough spaces present to convert to a tab
116→                // val spaceCount = asSpaces.length - (tabCount * tabWidth)
117→                // "\t".repeat(tabCount) + " ".repeat(spaceCount)
118→            }
119→        }
120→    }
121→
122→    private fun getTextAfterLastNewLine(text: String): String {
123→        val index = text.indexOfLast { it == '\n' }
124→        val indent =
125→            if (index == -1) {
126→                text
127→            } else {
128→                text.substring(index + 1, text.length)
129→            }
130→        return indent
131→    }
132→
133→    private fun String.replaceTabWithSpaces() = replace("\t", " ".repeat(tabWidth))
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Gets the indent level for given [text]. If [text] contains a new line, then only text after the last new line
137→     * is considered. This text may only contain spaces and tabs.
138→     *
139→     * Tabs in the [text] are first converted to spaces depending on the tab size of the indent style. For each set
140→     * of spaces that is equivalent to one tab is counted as indentation level. Space that are remaining because
141→     * they do not fill up an entire tab, are ignored silently.
142→     */
143→    public fun indentLevelFrom(text: String): Int {
144→        val indent = getTextAfterLastNewLine(text)
145→        require(indent.matches(TABS_AND_SPACES))
146→        return indent.replaceTabWithSpaces().length / tabWidth
147→    }
148→
149→    public fun containsUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Boolean = indentText.contains(unexpectedIndentChar)
150→
151→    public fun indexOfFirstUnexpectedIndentChar(indentText: String): Int = indentText.indexOfFirst { it == unexpectedIndentChar }
152→
153→    public val unexpectedIndentCharDescription: String =
154→        when (indentStyle) {
155→            SPACE -> "tab"
156→            TAB -> "space"
157→        }
158→
159→    public companion object {
160→        private val TABS_AND_SPACES = Regex("[ \t]*")
161→
162→        public val DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG: IndentConfig =
163→            IndentConfig(
164→                indentStyle = INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
165→                tabWidth = INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.defaultValue,
166→            )
167→    }
168→}

 51→/**

 53→ *
 54→ * ```

 68→@Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
 69→public class KtLintAssertThat(

 93→     * been set before, are silently overwritten with the new vale.

 95→    public fun withEditorConfigOverride(vararg properties: Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>): KtLintAssertThat {
 96→        editorConfigProperties.addAll(properties)
 97→
 98→        return this
 99→    }
100→

100→
101→    /**
102→     * Set the [EditorConfigOverride] "max_line_length" property based on the EOL Marker which is places at the first line of the code
103→     * sample. If the property has been set before via [withEditorConfigOverride] then that value is silently overwritten.
104→     *
105→     * Example of usage:
106→     * ```
107→     *  val code =
108→     *      """
109→     *      // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
110→     *      val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"

118→    public fun setMaxLineLength(): KtLintAssertThat {

122→            ?.indexOf(EOL_CHAR)

126→        return this
127→    }

277→    private fun code() =

279→            // Create a file containing the code and the given filepath. CodeFile can not be used here as it can not read successfully from
280→            // the Ktlint test file system.
281→            val path = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.resolve(filePath)
282→            KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.writeFile(path.pathString, code)
283→            Code.fromPath(path)
284→        } else {
285→            Code.fromSnippet(code, kotlinScript)

288→    public companion object {
289→        /**
290→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has extensions specifically
291→         * for testing KtLint rules.
292→         */
293→        public fun assertThatRule(provider: () -> Rule): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat()
294→
295→        /**
296→         * Creates an assertThat assertion function for the rule provided by [provider]. This assertion function has extensions specifically
297→         * for testing KtLint rules. The rules provided via [additionalRuleProviders] are only executed during the format phase of the test.
298→         * This means that the unit test only has to check the lint violations thrown by the rule for which the assertThat is created. But
299→         * the code is formatted by both the rule and the rules provided by [additionalRuleProviders] in the order as defined by the rule
300→         * definitions.
301→         */
302→
303→        public fun assertThatRule(
304→            provider: () -> Rule,
305→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
306→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
307→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat = RuleProvider { provider() }.assertThat(additionalRuleProviders, editorConfigProperties)
308→
309→        private fun RuleProvider.assertThat(
310→            additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
311→            editorConfigProperties: Set<Pair<EditorConfigProperty<*>, *>> = emptySet(),
312→        ): (String) -> KtLintAssertThat =
313→            { code ->
314→                KtLintAssertThat(
315→                    ruleProvider = this,
316→                    code = code,
317→                    additionalRuleProviders = additionalRuleProviders.toMutableSet(),
318→                    editorConfigProperties = editorConfigProperties.toMutableSet(),
319→                )
320→            }
321→
322→        /**
323→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
324→         */
325→        public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER: String = "Max line length marker:"
326→
327→        /**
328→         * See [setMaxLineLength] for intended usage.
329→         */
330→        public const val EOL_CHAR: Char = '#'
331→
332→        init {
333→            /* A unit test using the max line length marker typically looks like:
334→             * val code =
335→             *     """
336→             *     // $MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER                   $EOL_CHAR
337→             *     val fooooooooooooooo = "fooooooooooooooooooooo"
338→             *     """.trimIndent()
339→             * In order for this to work properly, the length of "$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER" must be identical to the length of the real value
340→             * of the constant MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.
341→             */
342→            require("$".plus(::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name).length == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.length) {
343→                "Length of the value of the string '${'$'}MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' must be identical to length of the value of the " +
344→                    "constant. Tests using this constant will not work as intended otherwise."
345→            }
346→        }
347→    }
348→}

350→/**
351→ * Immutable assertable. Once the first assertion is made on [KtLintAssertThat] it is converted to the [KtLintAssertThatAssertable] which
352→ * allows no further modifications of the internal state. This guarantees that all assertions operate on the same state.
353→ */
354→public class KtLintAssertThatAssertable(
355→    /** The provider of the rule which is the subject of the test, e.g. the rule for which the AssertThat is created. */
356→    private val ruleProvider: RuleProvider,
357→    private val code: Code,
358→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
359→    /**
360→     *  The rules which have to be executed in addition to the main rule when linting/formatting the code. Note that
361→     *  lint errors for those rules are suppressed.
362→     */
363→    private val additionalRuleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>,
364→) : AbstractAssert<KtLintAssertThatAssertable, String>(code.content, KtLintAssertThatAssertable::class.java) {
365→    private val ruleId = ruleProvider.createNewRuleInstance().ruleId
366→
367→    /**
368→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s caused by the rule associated with the KtLintAssertThat.
369→     *
370→     * Note: When linting succeeds without errors, formatting is also checked.
371→     */
372→    public fun hasNoLintViolations() {
373→        assertThat(lint().filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
374→
375→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
376→        val (actualFormattedCode, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
377→
378→        assertAll(
379→            {
380→                assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting)
381→                    .describedAs(
382→                        "LintViolations found by format while no lint errors were found during Lint. " +
383→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
384→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
385→                            } else {
386→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
387→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
388→                            },
389→                    )
390→                    .isEmpty()
391→            },
392→            {
393→                assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
394→                    .describedAs(
395→                        "Code is changed by format while no lint errors were found. " +
396→                            if (additionalRuleProviders.isEmpty()) {
397→                                "This is unexpected as no additional rule have been defined for this test."
398→                            } else {
399→                                "If this is caused by an additional rule added to the test, then remove that rule in case it " +
400→                                    "is always executed before the rule under test."
401→                            },
402→                    )
403→                    .isEqualTo(code.content)
404→            },
405→        )
406→    }
407→
408→    /**
409→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s for the given rule id.
410→     */
411→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsForRuleId(ruleId: RuleId): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
412→        val (_, lintErrorsWhenFormatting) = format()
413→
414→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })
415→            .describedAs("At least 1 lint violation was found for rule id '${ruleId.value}' while none were expected")
416→            .isEmpty()
417→
418→        return this
419→    }
420→
421→    /**
422→     * Asserts that the code does not contain any [LintViolation]s except in the additional formatting rules.
423→     *
424→     * Note that this method can and should be chained with [isFormattedAs] to verify whether the code is correctly formatted.
425→     *
426→     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample code, but the code
427→     * is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each other, a unit test cal still verify that
428→     * code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not violated.
429→     */
430→    public fun hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules(): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
431→        check(additionalRuleProviders.isNotEmpty()) {
432→            "hasNoLintViolationsExceptInAdditionalRules can only be used when additional rules have been added"
433→        }
434→
435→        val lintErrors = lint()
436→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterCurrentRuleOnly()).isEmpty()
437→        assertThat(lintErrors.filterAdditionalRulesOnly()).isNotEmpty
438→
439→        return this
440→    }
441→
442→    /**
443→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]. This is a sugar-coated version of [hasLintViolation] for the case that the
444→     * code contains exactly one lint violation.
445→     */
446→    public fun hasLintViolation(
447→        line: Int,
448→        col: Int,
449→        detail: String,
450→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
451→        hasLintViolations(
452→            LintViolation(
453→                line = line,
454→                col = col,
455→                detail = detail,
456→            ),
457→        )
458→
459→    /**
460→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation] caused by an additional rule. This is a sugar-coated version of
461→     * [hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules] for the case that the code contains exactly one lint violation.
462→     */
463→    public fun hasLintViolationForAdditionalRule(
464→        line: Int,
465→        col: Int,
466→        detail: String,
467→    ): KtLintAssertThatAssertable =
468→        hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(
469→            LintViolation(
470→                line = line,
471→                col = col,
472→                detail = detail,
473→            ),
474→        )
475→
476→    /**
477→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s which can be automatically corrected.
478→     */
479→    public fun hasLintViolations(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
480→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
481→
482→        val actualLintViolationFields =
483→            lint()
484→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
485→                .toLintViolationsFields()
486→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
487→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
488→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields())
489→        return this
490→    }
491→
492→    /**
493→     * Asserts that the code does contain given [LintViolation]s caused by additional rules and which can be automatically corrected.
494→     */
495→    public fun hasLintViolationsForAdditionalRules(vararg expectedErrors: LintViolation): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
496→        check(expectedErrors.isNotEmpty())
497→
498→        val actualLintViolationFields =
499→            lint()
500→                .filterAdditionalRulesOnly()
501→                .toLintViolationsFields()
502→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
503→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can be automatically corrected")
504→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedErrors.toLintViolationsFields())
505→        return this
506→    }
507→
508→    /**
509→     * Asserts that the code is formatted as given.
510→     */
511→    public fun isFormattedAs(formattedCode: String): KtLintAssertThatAssertable {
512→        check(formattedCode != code.content) {
513→            "Use '.hasNoLintViolations()' instead of '.isFormattedAs(<original code>)'"
514→        }
515→
516→        // Also format the code to be absolutely sure that codes does not get changed
517→        val (actualFormattedCode, _) = format()
518→
519→        assertThat(actualFormattedCode)
520→            .describedAs("Code is formatted as")
521→            .isEqualTo(formattedCode)
522→
523→        return this
524→    }
525→
526→    /**
527→     * Asserts that the code contains the given [LintViolation] which can not be automatically corrected.
528→     */
529→    public fun hasLintViolationWithoutAutoCorrect(
530→        line: Int,
531→        col: Int,
532→        detail: String,
533→    ): Unit =
534→        hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(
535→            LintViolation(
536→                line = line,
537→                col = col,
538→                detail = detail,
539→            ),
540→        )
541→
542→    /**
543→     * Asserts that the code does contain the given [LintViolation]s and that those violations can not be automatically corrected.
544→     */
545→    public fun hasLintViolationsWithoutAutocorrect(vararg expectedLintViolations: LintViolation) {
546→        check(expectedLintViolations.isNotEmpty())
547→
548→        val actualLintViolationFields =
549→            lint()
550→                .filterCurrentRuleOnly()
551→                .map {
552→                    LintViolationFields(
553→                        line = it.line,
554→                        col = it.col,
555→                        detail = it.detail,
556→                        canBeAutoCorrected = false,
557→                    )
558→                }.toTypedArray()
559→        val expectedLintViolationFields =
560→            expectedLintViolations
561→                .map {
562→                    LintViolationFields(
563→                        line = it.line,
564→                        col = it.col,
565→                        detail = it.detail,
566→                        canBeAutoCorrected = false,
567→                    )
568→                }
569→
570→        assertThat(actualLintViolationFields)
571→            .describedAs("Lint errors which can not be automatically corrected")
572→            .containsExactlyInAnyOrder(*expectedLintViolationFields.toTypedArray())
573→    }
574→
575→    private fun Array<out LintViolation>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> {
576→        return map {
577→            LintViolationFields(
578→                line = it.line,
579→                col = it.col,
580→                detail = it.detail,
581→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
582→            )
583→        }.distinct()
584→            .toTypedArray()
585→    }
586→
587→    private fun Set<LintError>.filterAdditionalRulesOnly() = filter { it.ruleId != ruleId }.toSet()
588→
589→    private fun Set<LintError>.filterCurrentRuleOnly() = filter { it.ruleId == ruleId }.toSet()
590→
591→    private fun Set<LintError>.toLintViolationsFields(): Array<LintViolationFields> =
592→        map {
593→            LintViolationFields(
594→                line = it.line,
595→                col = it.col,
596→                detail = it.detail,
597→                canBeAutoCorrected = it.canBeAutoCorrected,
598→            )
599→        }.distinct()
600→            .toTypedArray()

602→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
603→        val ruleProviders =
604→            setOf(ruleProvider)
605→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of the additional
606→                // rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
607→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)
608→        val editorConfigOverride =
609→            editorConfigOverride
610→                .enableExperimentalRules()
611→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)
612→        return KtLintRuleEngine(
613→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
614→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
615→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
616→        )
617→    }
618→
619→    private fun lint(): Set<LintError> {
620→        val lintErrors = mutableSetOf<LintError>()
621→        createKtLintRuleEngine().lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
622→        return lintErrors
623→    }
624→
625→    private fun format(): Pair<String, Set<LintError>> {
626→        val lintErrors = mutableSetOf<LintError>()
627→        val formattedCode = createKtLintRuleEngine().format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
628→        return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
629→    }
630→
631→    /**
632→     * Representation of the field of the [LintError] that should be identical. Note that no comparison can be made against the original
633→     * [LintError] as the [canBeAutoCorrected] flag is excluded from the hashcode.
634→     */
635→    private data class LintViolationFields(
636→        val line: Int,
637→        val col: Int,
638→        val detail: String,
639→        val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
640→    )
641→}

643→internal class MissingEolMarker :
644→    RuntimeException(
645→        """
646→        The first line of the provide code sample should contain text '$MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER' which is provided by the
647→        constant '${::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name}' and ends with the EOL_CHAR'$EOL_CHAR' provided by the constant
648→        '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.
649→        """.trimIndent(),
650→    )
651→
652→/**
653→ * Expectation of the [LintError]. Contrary to the [LintError] it does not contain the ruleId. The ruleId will be derived from the rule for
654→ * which the AssertThat was created.
655→ */
656→public data class LintViolation(
657→    val line: Int,
658→    val col: Int,
659→    val detail: String,
660→    val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean = true,
661→)
662→
663→/**
664→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already provided.
665→ */
666→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
667→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions =
668→        ruleProviders
669→            .asSequence()
670→            .map { ruleProvider ->
671→                ruleProvider
672→                    .createNewRuleInstance()
673→                    .ruleId
674→                    .ruleSetId
675→                    .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
676→            }.distinct()
677→            .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
678→            .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
679→            .toList()
680→            .toTypedArray()
681→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
682→}
683→
684→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
685→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)

412→    /**

417→
418→        assertThat(lintErrorsWhenFormatting.filter { it.ruleId == ruleId })

429→     *
430→     * This method can be used when the rule which is associated with the KtLintAssertThat is not violated by the sample code, but the code
431→     * is reformatted by the additional formatting rules. In case that rules are dependent on each other, a unit test cal still verify that
432→     * code is formatted correctly even when the rule under test is not violated.

596→        map {
597→            LintViolationFields(

606→    private fun createKtLintRuleEngine(): KtLintRuleEngine {
607→        val ruleProviders =
608→            setOf(ruleProvider)
609→                // Also run the additional rules as the main rule might have a VisitorModifier which requires one or more of the additional
610→                // rules to be loaded and enabled as well.
611→                .plus(additionalRuleProviders)
612→        val editorConfigOverride =
613→            editorConfigOverride
614→                .enableExperimentalRules()
615→                .extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders)
616→        return KtLintRuleEngine(
617→            ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
618→            editorConfigOverride = editorConfigOverride,
619→            fileSystem = KTLINT_TEST_FILE_SYSTEM.fileSystem,
620→        )
621→    }
622→
623→    private fun lint(): Set<LintError> {
624→        val lintErrors = mutableSetOf<LintError>()
625→        createKtLintRuleEngine().lint(code) { lintError -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
626→        return lintErrors
627→    }
628→
629→    private fun format(): Pair<String, Set<LintError>> {
630→        val lintErrors = mutableSetOf<LintError>()
631→        val formattedCode = createKtLintRuleEngine().format(code) { lintError, _ -> lintErrors.add(lintError) }
632→        return Pair(formattedCode, lintErrors)
633→    }

635→    /**
636→     * Representation of the field of the [LintError] that should be identical. Note that no comparison can be made against the original
637→     * [LintError] as the [canBeAutoCorrected] flag is excluded from the hashcode.
638→     */
639→    private data class LintViolationFields(
640→        val line: Int,
641→        val col: Int,
642→        val detail: String,
643→        val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean,
644→    )
645→}

651→        constant '${::MAX_LINE_LENGTH_MARKER.name}' and ends with the EOL_CHAR'$EOL_CHAR' provided by the constant
652→        '${::EOL_CHAR.name}' which indicates the last position that is allowed.
653→        """.trimIndent(),

656→/**
657→ * Expectation of the [LintError]. Contrary to the [LintError] it does not contain the ruleId. The ruleId will be derived from the rule for
658→ * which the AssertThat was created.
659→ */
660→public data class LintViolation(
661→    val line: Int,
662→    val col: Int,
663→    val detail: String,
664→    val canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean = true,
665→)
666→
667→/**
668→ * Enables the rule sets for the given set of [ruleProviders] unless the rule execution of that rule set was already provided.
669→ */
670→private fun EditorConfigOverride.extendWithRuleSetRuleExecutionsFor(ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider>): EditorConfigOverride {
671→    val ruleSetRuleExecutions =
672→        ruleProviders
673→            .asSequence()
674→            .map { ruleProvider ->
675→                ruleProvider
676→                    .createNewRuleInstance()
677→                    .ruleId
678→                    .ruleSetId
679→                    .createRuleSetExecutionEditorConfigProperty()
680→            }.distinct()
681→            .filter { editorConfigProperty -> this.properties[editorConfigProperty] == null }
682→            .map { it to RuleExecution.enabled }
683→            .toList()
684→            .toTypedArray()
685→    return this.plus(*ruleSetRuleExecutions)
686→}
687→
688→private fun EditorConfigOverride.enableExperimentalRules(): EditorConfigOverride =
689→    plus(EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY to RuleExecution.enabled)

1→# Changelog
 2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4→
 5→## Unreleased
 6→
 7→### Added
 8→
 9→* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)
10→
11→### Removed
12→
13→### Fixed
14→
15→* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
16→* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
17→* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
18→* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
19→* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
20→* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
21→* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
22→* Do not wrap a binary expression after an elvis operator in case the max line length is exceeded ([#2128](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2128))
23→* Fix indent of IS_EXPRESSION, PREFIX_EXPRESSION and POSTFIX_EXPRESSION in case it contains a linebreak `indent` [#2094](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2094)
24→* Add new experimental rule `function-literal`. This rule enforces the parameter list of a function literal to be formatted consistently. `function-literal` [#2121](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2121)
25→
26→### Changed
27→
28→* Change default code style to `ktlint_official`. Starting from ktlint `1.0` the default code style changes from `intellij_idea` (former `kotlin_official`) to `ktlint_official`. The `ktlint_official` codestyle adheres to the best parts of the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android Kotlint styleguid](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). It no longer is restricted to produce code compatible with the default IntelliJ IDEA formatter which lacks from bug which still are not resolved after multiple years.
29→* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
30→* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
31→* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
32→* As a part of public API stabilization, configure `binary-compatibility-validator` plugin for compile-time verification of binary compatibility with previous `ktlint` versions ([#2131](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2131))
33→
34→## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
35→
36→### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
37→
38→The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
39→
40→API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
41→
42→The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
43→
44→### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
45→
46→In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
47→
48→In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
49→
50→Based on the refactoring of the rules as provided by `ktlint-ruleset-standard` in Ktlint `0.49.x` the suggested refactoring is as follows:
